evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: PTB v20 async API, Application.builder().token().updater(None).build(), setWebhook with secret_token, X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header verification, Update.de_json all align. However, BaseSettings import from pydantic is Pydantic v1 syntax; in v2 it moved to pydantic-settings — minor inconsistency given pinned versions absent.
evidence.executability: Code is well-formed but ngrok is used as the primary local exposure mechanism (sandbox-infeasible per harness pattern). Also pydantic BaseSettings import will fail under modern Pydantic v2 without pydantic-settings dependency. Caps at 2.
evidence.completeness: (a) BotFather acquisition steps present; (b) pip install bash block present; (c) env var names listed in Settings class but no explicit .env template block; (d) backend app.py present; (e) N/A frontend (bot integration); (f) test command via curl POST present. Env vars defined in code consistently referenced. Missing standalone .env template block is light gap.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites core.telegram.org/bots/api (official Telegram docs), docs.python-telegram-bot.org, fastapi.tiangolo.com — official sources placed near relevant code sections. No fabricated URLs detected.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling — internal consistency strong, PTB v20 async patterns coherent. Ngrok usage triggers executability cap at 2. Pydantic BaseSettings import ambiguity noted.
